arts.ttl: EXCLUSIVE: Tottenham and Liverpool battle for Arsenal star’s brother
arts.sum: The younger brother of Arsenal's Jurrien Timber is expected to leave Feyenoord either in January or next summer, with both clubs keen to bolster their midfield options. Quinten Timber, the Feyenoord midfield dynamo, is attracting interest from Premier League giants Tottenham and Liverpool, FootballTransfers can reveal.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou reveals Cristian Romero apology over Tottenham criticism
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou confirms Cristian Romero has apologised for his criticism of the Tottenham hierarchy.

arts.ttl: Tottenham and player rule out January exit after transfer contact – Player ‘wants to remain’ at Spurs
arts.sum: Earlier today, Globo Esporte reported Fluminense have contacted Tottenham Hotspur’s Richarlison over a deal for the January transfer window. There was no mention of the 27-year-old or Tottenham’s stance on doing business with the Brazilian club. TNT Sports in Brazil now brings the latest on Richarlison and Tottenham’s stance for January. arts.ttl: “He’s not normal” – Chelsea star praised for Tottenham display, would have been in Golden Generation
arts.sum: Chelsea midfielder Roméo Lavia would have been in Belgium’s Golden Generation if he’d been born earlier. That’s according to analyst Filip Joos, via Voetbal Primeur, who was left very impressed by the midfielder’s display in the 4-3 win over Tottenham. arts.ttl: Insider Shares How Long Van De Ven Will be Out Injured for Tottenham
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur defender Micky van de Ven could be ruled out for up to three weeks after limping off in the 4-3 defeat to Chelsea.

arts.ttl: Neil Lennon warns Rangers 'destruction' is coming and he'll enjoy watching it if Tottenham turn on the style
arts.sum: Lennon reckons Rangers could be in trouble if Spurs turn it on at Ibrox tonight

arts.ttl: “That boy has quality” – Tottenham target backed to make it if he joins Spurs, former teammate signs praises
arts.sum: Feyenoord striker Anis Hadj Moussa absolutely has the quality to succeed at Tottenham if they were to move for him in the coming months. That’s according to his former teammate Henk Dijkhuizen via Voetbal Primeur, who believes the 22-year-old has everything to succeed at a higher level.
